Implements the URIError prototype object for the ECMAScript engine in the Pebble firmware.

# Purpose
The code defines components related to the ECMAScript (JavaScript) engine, specifically focusing on the URIError prototype. It includes several header files that provide memory allocation, built-in object management, type conversion, exception handling, garbage collection, and other helper functions necessary for ECMAScript operations. The code is part of a larger system that implements ECMAScript built-in objects and their prototypes.

The conditional compilation directive `#ifndef CONFIG_DISABLE_ERROR_BUILTINS` indicates that the code for the URIError prototype is only included if error built-ins are not disabled. The code uses a template mechanism to include internal routines for the URIError prototype by defining `BUILTIN_INC_HEADER_NAME` and `BUILTIN_UNDERSCORED_ID`, which are used to include the appropriate internal routines template. This approach allows for modular and configurable inclusion of built-in error handling functionalities within the ECMAScript engine.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `ecma-alloc.h`
- `ecma-builtins.h`
- `ecma-conversion.h`
- `ecma-exceptions.h`
- `ecma-gc.h`
- `ecma-globals.h`
- `ecma-helpers.h`
- `ecma-objects.h`
- `ecma-string-object.h`
- `ecma-try-catch-macro.h`
- `jrt.h`
- `ecma-builtins-internal.h`
- `ecma-builtin-internal-routines-template.inc.h`
